message:
fix(modal/dialog): honor modal_config.hit_id + respect explicit modal size constraints

Two related bugs in how Dialog interacts with its ModalConfig, both
surfaced by the focus/hit-test work in the previous commit.

## modal_config() overwrote size but forgot to carry hit_id

Both `Dialog::modal_config(config)` (the instance setter) and
`DialogBuilder::modal_config(config)` (the builder setter) were
assigning `self.config.modal_config = config` but leaving
`self.hit_id` pointing at whatever the default was. The dialog's
own hit-test machinery reads `self.hit_id` to filter incoming
events, so a caller who set a specific hit_id via
`ModalConfig { hit_id, .. }` would never actually receive events
targeted at that id — they would get routed to the default hit_id
and silently dropped.

Fix: both setters now also copy `config.hit_id` into `self.hit_id`
before assigning the full modal_config. Straightforward
"keep the shadow state in sync" fix; no behavior change for
callers that leave the default hit_id alone.

## effective_size_constraints preserves caller-provided sizes

`StatefulWidget for Dialog::render` was unconditionally overwriting
the modal_config's size with:

    ModalSizeConstraints::new()
        .min_width(30).max_width(60)
        .min_height(content_height)
        .max_height(content_height + 4)

which meant a caller who had built a ModalConfig with bespoke
width/height limits (for e.g. a full-screen confirm, or a very
narrow toast-like dialog) saw their constraints silently replaced.

Extract a new private method `effective_size_constraints(content_height)`
that defaults each axis independently:

    - if neither min_width nor max_width is set, apply (30, 60).
    - if neither min_height nor max_height is set, apply
      (content_height, content_height + 4).
    - otherwise, honor whatever the caller already set on that
      axis and leave it alone.

This preserves the Dialog's nice default dimensions for the
common case (no caller-supplied constraints) while letting
callers override either axis explicitly when they need to.

## Test infrastructure

Add a `hit_bounds(frame, expected)` test helper that walks the
frame's hit map and returns the bounding rectangle for the given
`(HitId, HitRegion, data)` triple. Used by a new test that
registers a custom hit_id via `modal_config`, renders the dialog,
and asserts the bounds cover the expected region — pinning the
modal_config.hit_id fix above.
